\noindent In \ttblue{R} we use the function \ttblue{pnorm},
that uses the arguments \ttblue{q} for the
sampling result that we evaluate, \ttblue{mean}
for the population mean, and \ttblue{sd} for the
standard deviation of the mean.


\par\addvspace{\topsep}
\begingroup
\fvset{listparameters={%
  \setlength{\topsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\partopsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\parsep}{0pt}%

\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
norm.cdf(x= 1012, loc= 1030, scale= 115.26 / sqrt(200))
\end{Verbatim}
